action.reasoning: Observation: We have ~25 minutes left and 12 iterations. The best-ever eval score is rmse_z500=1018.63 from iteration 5. The retrained model (iter 14/17) scored 1032 despite lower val_loss, suggesting the final-epoch model or a different seed may generalize better on the hidden test set. We have an unused strong checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt (ensemble m1, val_loss=0.112) that was never evaluated alone. Analysis: Evaluating best.pt costs no training time and could yield a competitive score. Simultaneously, we should train a fresh model with the proven architecture (hidden=256, 12 blocks) for exactly 5 epochs—matching iteration 5’s training length—saving every epoch so we can pick the final epoch if validation loss early-stops again. We also harden predict.py to load normalization stats from the data directory if missing from the checkpoint. Decision: Update predict.py for robustness, rewrite train.py to save per-epoch snapshots without early stopping, verify best.pt loads, start training, and submit best.pt for eval immediately.
action.command: python -c "import torch; from model import WeatherModel; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); args=ckpt.get('args',{}); model=WeatherModel(in_ch=6,out_ch=2,hidden=args.get('hidden',256),num_blocks=args.get('num_blocks',12),dropout=args.get('dropout',0.0),residual=args.get('residual',False)); state={k.replace('_orig_mod.',''):v for k,v in ckpt['model'].items()}; model.load_state_dict(state); print('best.pt load OK')" && python train.py --epochs 5 --batch_size 128 --hidden 256 --num_blocks 12 --dropout 0.0 --weight_decay 0.0 --save_path checkpoints/run.pt
